verification_command passed: test -f docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md && grep -q ADR-004 docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md && grep -q SD-004 docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md && grep -q ddx-da11a34a docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
rationale: Bead ddx-3b06699f asks for a TD on axon-backed bead-tracker integration.
That TD already exists at docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
(committed by sibling bead ddx-eb7d5981, commit 5740627f). It satisfies every AC:

AC1 (TD under docs/): TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md.
AC2 (coverage):
  - data model mapping → "Schema mapping" + "Schema artifacts" sections
  - claim/lock semantics → "Concurrency model" (OCC via expected_version, multi-worker fairness)
  - archive policy → migration §2 ingests .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl,
    .ddx/attachments/*; "What this design fixes" notes ADR-004 archive split is subsumed
    by the two-collection model (ddx_beads + ddx_bead_events per D3)
  - JSONL interchange → operation-mapping rows for `ddx bead export` / `ddx bead import`,
    cites FEAT-004 round-trip + schema_compat_test.go
  - failure modes → D4 offline posture, `ddx doctor` diagnostic with four named failure modes,
    test plan §5–§6
  - migration story → "Migration plan" (6-step, idempotent, with rollback bak file),
    references B_MIGRATE child bead ddx-155204fd
AC3 (cites): ADR-004 (lines 5, 15, 213, 266, 304), SD-004 (lines 6, 15, 73, 306),
  ddx-da11a34a (lines 156, 247, 264, 274).
AC4 (open questions): "Open questions for the Axon team" §1 (server-side ready aggregation)
  and §2 (tsnet alignment with ADR-006); v1-policy table also flags deferred decisions
  (schema versioning, ready query, backup/DR) explicitly revisitable.

Producing a duplicate TD-### file would split the design surface and create drift.
No code or doc changes warranted on this attempt.
